content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a browser-based UI for the tc24r compiler using Rust/Yew/WASM. Phase 1: scaffold Yew project, embed tc24r as WASM. Phase 2: editor component with syntax highlighting. Phase 3: compile-and-run workflow in browser.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-research-and-scaffold [production]: Study the tc24r compiler at ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r (read README, CLAUD...
  [x] 002-embed-tc24r [legacy]: Compile tc24r core to WASM, integrate into Yew app
  [ ] 003-editor [legacy]: Add code editor component with syntax highlighting <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 004-compile-run [legacy]: Wire compile and run workflow in browser
  [ ] 005-output-display [legacy]: Show assembler output and emulator execution results

=== CURRENT STEP: 003-editor [legacy] ===
Status: pending
Description: Add code editor component with syntax highlighting

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

content.text: Let me check the current state of the project before adding syntax highlighting.

file.content:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
    <title>web-tc24r — COR24 Compiler</title>
    <link data-trunk rel="rust" data-wasm-opt="z" />
    <style>
        * { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
        body {
            font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
            background: #1e1e2e;
            color: #cdd6f4;
            min-height: 100vh;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
</body>
</html>

content.text: Now let me read the compiler.rs to understand the full picture.
I'll build a code editor with syntax highlighting using the overlay technique (transparent textarea over highlighted `<pre>`). Let me create the highlighter and editor component.
Now update `main.rs` to use the editor component and add the necessary web-sys features.

content.text:
Syntax highlighting is working well. I can see:
- **Comments** in gray (`// COR24 C — UART hello + LED`)
- **Keywords** (`void`, `return`) in mauve/purple
- **Types** (`int`, `char`) in blue
- **Numbers** (`0xFF0100`, `72`, etc.) in peach/orange
- **Function calls** (`putc`, `led_on`, `main`) in yellow

Let me verify the editor is interactive by clicking in it and testing the compile button.

content.text: Everything works — the editor has syntax highlighting, the compile & run pipeline still functions, and all three panels display correctly. Let me stop the dev server and commit.

Add code editor with C syntax highlighting

Replace plain textarea with overlay editor component: transparent
textarea over a highlighted <pre> for editable syntax-colored code.
Highlights keywords, types, numbers, strings, comments, preprocessor
directives, and function calls using the Catppuccin Mocha palette.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
